commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Diamante Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Diamante's 6A12 and 6G7x V6 engines are generally robust, but UK grey import owners commonly face timing belt failures, low-RPM misfires, and valve noise issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to decide whether repair or replacement offers the best value for your Diamante.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Mitsubishi Diamante Engines Suffer Catastrophic Damage
problems.rootCause: The 6A12/6G7x family are interference engines; a snapped timing belt causes pistons to strike valves, bending both.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Belt Replacement - Before failure occurs
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump, seals
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory service interval; recommended at 60k miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After belt snap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded components, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: New-engine reliability; only viable option after valve damage

problems.recommendation: If your Diamante is still running and the belt is overdue, invest in preventative replacement. If the belt has snapped, cylinder head damage is almost certain - a rebuilt engine is the only cost-effective solution for grey imports.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6A12 and 6G73 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Diamante

problems.h4: Low-RPM Misfire - Why Mitsubishi Diamante Engines Hesitate Below 2000 RPM
problems.rootCause: Vacuum solenoid MR450086 or camshaft position sensor failures disrupt ignition timing at low engine speeds.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty CMP sensor or vacuum solenoid, clear codes
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves misfire if caught early; parts readily available from JDM suppliers

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - If damage escalated
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit addressing root cause
repairOptions.longevity: Only needed if misfire caused secondary damage (rare)

problems.recommendation: Start with diagnostic testing of the CMP sensor and vacuum solenoid. These are common, affordable fixes on Diamante V6 engines. Only consider replacement if misfire has caused catalytic converter damage or persistent rough running.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Diamante 6G73 engine replacement from UK specialists

problems.h4: Valve Noise at Start-Up - Hydraulic Lash Adjuster Issues on Mitsubishi Diamante Engines
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic lash adjusters lose prime or wear, causing tapping noise on cold start that may diminish as oil circulates.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, clean or replace adjusters, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if noise is isolated; labour-intensive on V6

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - If wear is widespread
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded valvetrain components
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if multiple components show wear or high mileage

problems.recommendation: If the noise is mild and disappears after warm-up, monitor oil pressure and use manufacturer-spec oil. If noise persists or worsens, a rebuilt engine often offers better long-term value than piecemeal valvetrain repairs on a grey import.
problems.cta: Compare reconditioned Mitsubishi Diamante V6 engine prices

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - Valve Seal Wear on High-Mileage Mitsubishi Diamante Engines
problems.rootCause: Rubber valve stem seals harden with age/heat, allowing oil to enter combustion chambers and burn.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve covers, replace seals without full head removal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if consumption is moderate and engine otherwise healthy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - If rings also worn
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded seals and rings
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if blue smoke is heavy or compression is low

problems.recommendation: Conduct a compression test first. If compression is good and oil use is under 1L/1,000 miles, valve seal replacement may suffice. For heavier consumption or high-mileage LS models, a rebuilt engine delivers better reliability and resale value.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Mitsubishi Diamante LS engine options with warranty

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Diamante
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing a snapped timing belt, persistent misfire, or valve noise, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Diamante specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Diamante Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Diamante and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Diamante Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Diamante's production run, organised so UK grey import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Diamante debuted with the 6A12 2.0L V6, establishing the engine family that would dominate UK grey import replacements for over a decade.
keyChanges: Mitsubishi Diamante launched as JDM executive sedan
6A12 SOHC 24v V6 introduced as standard engine
Early models featured distributor ignition and mechanical fuel injection
Australian production began for right-hand drive exports
knownFor: Robust V6 design with good JDM parts availability
Timing belt service critical (interference engine)
Distributor ignition failures on high-mileage examples
Strong foundation for later engine developments
years.cta: Get quotes for 1990-1993 Mitsubishi Diamante replacement engines

years.description: The facelift introduced the 6A12 DOHC variant for VR trim and refined the 2.0L V6 across the range, increasing UK grey import appeal.
keyChanges: 6A12 DOHC introduced for performance VR trim
Espada luxury trim launched with enhanced equipment
Minor chassis and interior updates for facelift models
Australian-built units began arriving in UK as grey imports
knownFor: DOHC variant offers higher output but similar reliability
Hydraulic lash adjuster noise more noticeable on DOHC
Vacuum solenoid failures begin appearing on higher-mileage units
Excellent parts interchangeability between SOHC/DOHC blocks
years.cta: Compare rebuilt 6A12 Mitsubishi Diamante engine prices
years.ticker: Diamante misfire under 2000 RPM • 6A12 timing belt replacement • VR engine compatibility

years.description: The all-new second generation introduced the 6G73 2.5L V6 as the new mainstream engine, with the premium LS trim gaining the 3.5L 6G74.
keyChanges: New platform with updated styling and chassis
LS trim introduced with 6G74 3.5L V6 and MIVEC
Wagon body style launched, popular with UK importers
knownFor: 6G73 offers strong torque and refined cruising
Low-RPM misfire issues linked to CMP sensor/vacuum solenoid
LS 6G74 delivers performance but watch for oil consumption
Wagon body style highly sought after in UK grey import market
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997-2001 Mitsubishi Diamante replacement engines
years.ticker: 6G73 valve noise at start-up • Diamante LS oil consumption • Wagon engine compatibility

years.description: Final production years saw refinements to the 6G73 and 6G74 engines, with improved electronics and emissions compliance for later grey imports.
keyChanges: Minor updates to engine management and emissions systems
LS trim received enhanced equipment and MIVEC refinement
Production ended in 2005; UK grey import supply shifted to used/JDM sources
Parts availability stabilised via specialist import networks
knownFor: Late-model units benefit from refined electronics
Timing belt service remains critical on all variants
Strong enthusiast following supports parts availability
Ideal candidates for rebuilt engine upgrades addressing known weaknesses
years.cta: Find Mitsubishi Diamante engine supply & fit deals for late models

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Mitsubishi Diamante was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Mitsubishi Diamante specialists.

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Diamante Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Where can I buy a Mitsubishi Diamante engine in the UK?
items.answer: UK owners source Diamante engines via specialist JDM importers, breaker networks, and EngineMarket's comparison service connecting you with vetted suppliers.
keyPoints: Diamante was never officially sold new in the UK by Mitsubishi
All engines are sourced from Japanese or Australian grey imports
EngineMarket compares prices from 100+ UK specialists instantly
Verify engine code (6A12, 6G73, etc.) before ordering
Request compression test results and warranty terms
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Diamante engine suppliers UK now

items.question: What engines fit a Mitsubishi Diamante?
items.answer: The 6A12, 6G73 and 6G74 are direct fits; 6A13 compatibility requires verification of mounts and electronics before purchase.
keyPoints: 6A12 fits all first-gen Diamante variants (1990-1996)
6G73 is standard for second-gen 25V and Wagon models
6G74 is exclusive to LS trim (1997-2005)
Galant/Legnum engines may fit but confirm mounting points
Always cross-reference chassis code and ECU compatibility
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi Diamante engine compatibility guide

items.question: How much to replace a Mitsubishi Diamante engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Diamante engines range from £950 for 6A12 units to £4,200 for 6G74 LS engines, plus £450-£950 for supply & fit labour.
keyPoints: Used JDM engines start around £650-£1,900 supply-only
Rebuilt 6A12/6G73 units typically £1,100-£2,800
LS 6G74 rebuilt engines command £1,800-£4,200
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£950 depending on location
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Get a tailored Mitsubishi Diamante engine replacement quote

items.question: Is a Diamante engine the same as a Galant?
items.answer: Many Diamante engines share architecture with Galant/Legnum, but mounting points, ECU and ancillaries often differ - verify before swapping.
keyPoints: 6A12 and 6G73 blocks are shared across Mitsubishi platforms
Engine mounts, wiring harnesses and ECU may not interchange
Transmission bellhousing patterns usually match within family
Always confirm variant-specific fitment with your supplier
EngineMarket specialists verify compatibility before dispatch

items.question: Why is my Diamante misfiring at low RPM?
items.answer: Low-RPM misfire on 6G73/6G74 engines is commonly caused by vacuum solenoid MR450086 or camshaft position sensor failure.
keyPoints: Fault codes P0300-P0306 indicate cylinder misfire
Vacuum solenoid failure disrupts idle air control
CMP sensor issues cause timing errors below 2000 RPM
Replace faulty sensor/solenoid before considering engine rebuild
Diagnostic testing costs £80-£150 at specialist garages
items.cta: Diagnose Mitsubishi Diamante misfire with UK specialists

items.question: Do you warranty JDM import engines?
items.answer: Yes - every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Compression test results provided before dispatch
Warranty valid at any UK garage, not just supplying specialist
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Claim process managed via EngineMarket support team

items.question: Will a 6A13 fit my Diamante?
items.answer: The 6A13 is not a guaranteed direct fit; verify mounting points, ECU compatibility and ancillary locations before ordering.
keyPoints: 6A13 shares displacement with 6G73 but different architecture
Engine mounts and transmission flange may not align
ECU and wiring harness likely require adaptation
Most UK specialists recommend 6G73 as safer replacement
EngineMarket compatibility check prevents wrong orders
items.cta: Confirm Mitsubishi Diamante 6A13 fitment before buying

items.question: How long does a Diamante engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Diamante V6 engines regularly exceed 150,000 miles; timing belt service at 60k miles is critical to avoid catastrophic failure.
keyPoints: 6A12 and 6G73 are robust designs when serviced correctly
Timing belt replacement every 60k miles prevents valve damage
Use manufacturer-spec oil to protect hydraulic lash adjusters
Address misfires promptly to avoid catalytic converter damage
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can exceed original lifespan
items.cta: Plan Mitsubishi Diamante engine maintenance schedule

items.question: Can I fit a Diamante engine myself?
items.answer: While mechanically possible, most UK garages decline grey import work; EngineMarket's supply & fit service connects you with experienced specialists.
keyPoints: Diamante engine swaps require JDM wiring/ECU knowledge
Many mainstream garages lack grey import experience
Supply & fit bundles save time and ensure warranty validity
Labour costs £450-£950 depending on location and complexity
Request itemised quote separating engine cost and fitting
items.cta: Book Mitsubishi Diamante supply & fit with vetted specialists

items.question: What causes 6A12 timing belt failure?
items.answer: Age, heat cycling and inadequate tensioner maintenance cause 6A12 timing belt failure; replacement every 60k miles prevents catastrophic valve damage.
keyPoints: 6A12 is an interference engine - snapped belt bends valves
Tensioner wear allows belt slap and premature failure
Water pump replacement recommended during belt service
Use OEM-spec belts and tensioners for reliability
EngineMarket rebuilt units include upgraded timing components
